wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the Plexus Canvas project. Explain its purpose as a real-time interactive particle network visualization tool built with vanilla JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Describe the core concept of rendering dynamic 'plexus' networks on an HTML canvas with live parameter adjustments. Highlight key features including interactive controls, presets, JSON import/export, PNG export, shareable URLs, and keyboard shortcuts. Document the event-driven architecture, Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern for performance, and spatial indexing (Grid/Quadtree). Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical insights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'plexus', 'spatialIndex', and 'config.on'. Provide practical examples of use cases like generative art, educational physics simulations, and creative exploration. Illustrate the relationship between major components like the rendering engine, configuration system, and UI controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the Configuration Schema. Detail the complete JSON structure used to define particle network visualizations, including particles, edges, forces, style, interaction, and performance sections. Explain each configuration field, its data type, default value, validation rules, and functional impact on the visualization. Document how the schema is initialized with defaults and updated via the event-driven system. Provide examples of valid configurations and demonstrate how to programmatically modify settings. Address common issues such as invalid values, out-of-range parameters, and schema evolution. Include performance considerations when applying large-scale configuration changes and describe how changes propagate through the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animation Loop
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the animation loop sub-component. Explain the implementation of the rendering loop using requestAnimationFrame, including how it integrates with the browser's refresh cycle for optimal performance. Document the optional FPS capping mechanism (30/60/120/Off) and its impact on battery life and visual smoothness. Describe the time-step management system and delta time calculation used to ensure consistent motion regardless of frame rate fluctuations. Illustrate how the engine handles pause/resume states and synchronizes updates with rendering. Include examples from the codebase showing loop control logic and event-driven toggling (e.g., via keyboard shortcuts). Address common issues such as frame skipping, time drift, and how the soft-cap mechanism helps maintain target frame rates on lower-end hardware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Module Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the module structure of plexus-canvas. Describe the separation of concerns across the src/ directory including render/, state/, ui/, utils/, and io/ modules. Explain the role of main.js as the application bootstrap that initializes and wires together configuration, rendering engine, UI controls, and import/export functionality. Document the single-responsibility principle applied in each module and how they interact through well-defined interfaces. Include a component diagram showing module dependencies and initialization order. Highlight the benefits of vanilla JavaScript modularity without bundlers and how the structure supports maintainability and extension. Provide examples from the codebase showing how modules are imported and instantiated.

wiki_catalogs.name: Control Panel
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the control panel UI component. Explain the implementation of the accordion-based layout with collapsible sections for Particles, Edges, Forces & Motion, Colors & Style, Interaction, Performance, and Presets/Import/Export. Describe how panel.js dynamically generates form controls (sliders, inputs, buttons) based on the configuration schema from config.js. Document the two-way binding between UI controls and the central configuration state, including event listeners that trigger 'change' events. Include examples of how new controls can be added by extending the config schema. Address responsive behavior below 900px where the panel reflows below the canvas. Provide guidance on customizing the panel structure and styling via CSS variables.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event System API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the event-driven configuration system. Detail the config.on('change', callback) method, including parameter specifications: 'path' (string representing the configuration key path) and 'value' (the new value being set). Explain how developers can subscribe to configuration changes to reactively update UI components or external systems. Provide practical examples showing event listener registration and removal. Document edge cases such as rapid-fire events and initial state emission. Include guidance on avoiding memory leaks through proper listener cleanup. Address performance considerations when handling high-frequency updates and recommend debounce patterns where appropriate. Explain integration with custom modules that depend on real-time configuration updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on onboarding new users and developers. Explain how to clone the repository, install dependencies using npm, and start the development server with 'npm run dev'. Provide step-by-step instructions for setting up the local environment including Node.js version requirements. Include a basic usage example showing how to launch the application and interact with the default visualization. Document the minimal prerequisites and installation process exactly as specified in the README. Address common setup issues such as missing dependencies or incorrect Node.js versions. Explain the zero-build nature of the project allowing direct opening of index.html. Make the content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical detail for troubleshooting setup problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Presets
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Presets system. Explain the implementation of the five built-in presets (Neon Breeze, Cosmic Web, Wireframe, Storm, Minimal) and how they provide starting points for visualization configurations. Document the getByName function interface and its role in loading preset configurations. Describe how presets are structured as JSON objects that override default settings. Include code examples showing how to retrieve and apply presets programmatically. Explain the relationship between presets and the main configuration system, including how preset values trigger change events. Address common usage patterns and potential issues when extending or modifying the preset system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Canvas Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the canvas management system. Explain how the canvas element is initialized, sized, and scaled based on container dimensions and HiDPI displays using devicePixelRatio detection. Document the pixelRatioMode setting that allows manual override of scaling behavior. Describe the resizing logic that responds to window or container changes while preserving aspect ratio and performance. Cover the background clearing strategies: full clear versus ghost trail effect through partial opacity redraw, and how these are controlled via configuration. Detail the coordination between the rendering engine and plexus module in drawing operations, including canvas context state management and restoration. Provide practical examples from the codebase showing context setup, scaling transforms, and cleanup routines. Address performance implications of different clearing methods and resolution scaling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Event-Driven Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the event-driven architecture centered around the config object. Explain how the config module acts as the central state management hub using an observer pattern with on('change', callback) subscriptions. Document how UI inputs trigger configuration updates, which then emit events to notify rendering, physics, and export systems. Illustrate with code examples the registration and invocation of event listeners across different components such as the control panel, renderer, and preset system. Describe the advantages of decoupling UI from logic and enabling extensibility through custom event handlers. Address common issues like event listener memory leaks and best practices for event cleanup. Include performance considerations when broadcasting frequent updates during animation loops.

wiki_catalogs.name: Keyboard Shortcuts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the keyboard shortcut system. Document all available shortcuts: Space (play/pause animation), R (soft reset), Shift+R (hard reset), S (save as PNG), [ and ] (decrease/increase particle count), and 1/2/3 (preset selection). Explain how event listeners in panel.js capture keydown events and map them to corresponding actions in the configuration and rendering systems. Provide code examples showing how shortcuts interact with the config and render modules. Address accessibility benefits and user experience improvements from keyboard navigation. Include troubleshooting guidance for common issues like focus loss and browser interception of shortcuts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Export Functions API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for all data export and import functions. Cover exportJSON(config) which serializes the configuration object into a JSON string, importJSON(text) that parses and validates JSON input to restore configuration state, savePNG(canvas) which triggers a downloadable PNG image of the current canvas state using optional file-saver library, and buildShareURL(config) that generates a compact, encoded URL containing the full configuration for sharing. For each function, specify parameters, return types, and possible error conditions (e.g., invalid JSON format, canvas security restrictions). Provide code examples demonstrating usage in both browser console and integrated scripts. Include error handling strategies for malformed inputs and security considerations around DOM-based XSS from untrusted JSON. Document performance implications of large configuration exports and browser download limitations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the technical foundation of Plexus Canvas. Detail the use of vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+), HTML5 Canvas API, and CSS for styling with potential Tailwind CSS usage. Emphasize the framework-free architecture and lack of build requirements. Document the optional dependency on file-saver for enhanced download functionality. Explain the rationale behind choosing vanilla JS for performance and simplicity. Cover the use of modern JavaScript features like modules, classes, and event handling. Include information about browser compatibility and devicePixelRatio handling for HiDPI displays. Address how the technology stack supports the project's performance goals of maintaining 60 FPS with 1000-1500 particles on mid-range machines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Import and Export
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Import and Export functionality. Cover JSON serialization and deserialization processes for saving and loading configurations. Explain localStorage persistence mechanism and its automatic state recovery behavior. Document the URL-based state sharing system that uses base64-encoded configurations to create shareable links. Describe the PNG export feature and its dependency on optional file-saver library. Provide code examples for each export method (exportJSON, savePNG, buildShareURL) and import workflows. Address common issues such as data corruption during transfer, browser storage limits, and cross-origin restrictions. Include performance considerations for handling large configuration files and security implications of executing imported configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rendering Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the rendering optimization techniques. Explain the batched edge rendering approach that minimizes canvas context calls by grouping all edges into a single beginPath and stroke operation, significantly improving draw performance. Describe how particle rendering leverages Structure of Arrays (SoA) for cache-efficient iteration. Cover the spatial indexing integration (Grid/Quadtree) that reduces connection calculations by limiting neighbor searches to local regions. Document the FPS soft-cap mechanism that dynamically adjusts workloads to maintain target frame rates on underpowered devices. Include code examples showing the rendering pipeline's optimized loops and conditional logic paths. Address the trade-offs between visual fidelity and performance, such as edge count limits and jitter reduction at high particle counts. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common rendering issues like flickering, lag, or memory bloat.

wiki_catalogs.name: Structure of Arrays (SoA)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Structure of Arrays (SoA) pattern implemented in the particle system. Explain how particle properties (x, y, vx, vy) are stored in separate Float32Arrays instead of objects to improve cache locality and computational efficiency. Contrast SoA with Array of Structures (AoS) and detail the performance benefits in tight rendering loops with thousands of particles. Use concrete examples from plexus.js showing how position updates, force calculations, and spatial indexing leverage SoA for optimized memory access. Discuss the trade-offs in code readability versus runtime performance. Provide guidance on when to apply SoA in similar visualization projects and how new properties should be integrated into the existing SoA layout.

wiki_catalogs.name: Accessibility and Responsive Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for accessibility and responsive design features. Explain how the UI supports keyboard navigation, semantic HTML structure, labeled form controls, and title attributes for improved screen reader compatibility. Document the responsive layout that switches from side-by-side (canvas + panel) to stacked (canvas above panel) on screens below 900px. Describe the implementation of dark/light theme toggling using CSS variables and persistence via localStorage. Include information about DPI scaling for high-resolution displays and touch-friendly control sizing. Provide best practices for maintaining accessibility when extending the UI and testing responsive behavior across devices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Preset System API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the visualization preset system. Focus on the getByName(name) function that retrieves a predefined configuration by its string identifier. Document the expected input (preset name string), return value (configuration object or null if not found), and behavior on invalid inputs. List all built-in presets (e.g., 'default', 'dense', 'sparse', 'gravity') and their visual characteristics. Provide examples of how to load and apply presets programmatically. Explain how developers can extend the preset registry with custom configurations. Include error handling guidance for undefined preset names and version compatibility issues when presets evolve. Address use cases such as initializing the application with a specific preset or allowing users to switch between styles dynamically.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the core application structure. Describe the high-level design with its modular architecture separating concerns into rendering, state management, UI, and utilities. Document the key architectural patterns: Event-Driven Architecture through config.on('change'), Structure of Arrays (SoA) for particle data storage in Float32Arrays, and the Observer Pattern for state change propagation. Explain the component interactions between main.js (bootstrap), config.js (central state), panel.js (UI binding), engine.js (animation loop), and plexus.js (particle logic). Illustrate the data flow from user input through configuration changes to visual updates. Include system context diagrams showing module relationships and data flow. Address the trade-offs of using vanilla JS without frameworks and the performance benefits of the chosen architectural patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the interaction flow between key components in plexus-canvas. Start with user input in the UI panel binding to config state changes, propagating through event emissions to the rendering engine and particle system. Detail how engine.js drives the animation loop using requestAnimationFrame and updates plexus.js with time deltas. Explain how plexus.js computes particle positions and edge connections using spatial indexing, then renders results to the canvas. Show how config state also enables export functions and URL sharing. Include a sequence diagram illustrating the full data flow from input to render. Address debugging strategies for broken update chains and performance bottlenecks in component communication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the configuration management system. Detail the JSON schema structure with its particles, edges, forces, style, interaction, and performance sections. Explain the config.js implementation with its validation, default values, and event emission system (config.on). Document the preset system with getByName functionality and the five built-in presets (Neon Breeze, Cosmic Web, Wireframe, Storm, Minimal). Cover the import/export functionality including JSON serialization/deserialization, localStorage persistence, and URL-based state sharing with base64-encoded configurations. Provide examples of valid configuration objects and demonstrate how to programmatically modify settings. Address common issues like invalid values and schema versioning. Include performance considerations for handling large configuration updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Rendering Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the rendering subsystem. Describe the animation loop implementation using requestAnimationFrame with optional FPS capping (30/60/120/Off). Explain the time-step management and delta time handling for smooth motion. Detail the HiDPI support through devicePixelRatio detection and manual pixelRatioMode settings. Document the canvas management including resizing behavior and background clearing strategies (full vs. ghost trail). Cover the rendering optimization techniques like batched edge drawing with single beginPath/stroke calls. Illustrate how the engine coordinates with the plexus module for particle updates and drawing. Address performance monitoring and the soft-cap mechanism for maintaining target frame rates on lower-end hardware.

wiki_catalogs.name: Particle System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the particle system component. Thoroughly explain the Structure of Arrays (SoA) implementation using Float32Arrays for x, y, vx, vy coordinates and velocities. Document the particle update algorithms including noise-based motion (Perlin/sinusoidal), gravity simulation toward the center, drag/damping effects, and boundary reflection behaviors. Cover the spawn area configurations (full, ellipse, ring, rect) and position reset mechanisms (soft/hard). Explain the jitter parameter and its effect on particle movement. Include concrete examples from the codebase showing how particle positions are updated each frame. Address performance considerations for large particle counts and cache efficiency benefits of SoA over AoS. Provide guidance on tuning parameters for different visual effects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Edge System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the edge system component. Explain the distance-based connection logic where edges form between particles within maxDistance threshold. Document the maxEdgesPerNode limitation and its performance implications. Detail the visual styling options including lineWidth, lineOpacity, and blendMode (normal, lighten, screen, overlay). Cover the three edge color modes: static, byDistance (interpolated through gradient), and byVelocity (based on speed magnitude). Explain the batched rendering approach using a single beginPath with multiple moveTo/lineTo calls for optimal performance. Address the spatial indexing integration (Grid/Quadtree) that accelerates edge detection. Provide examples of how edge parameters create different visual aesthetics from subtle wireframes to dense networks.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the UI components and interaction model. Describe the two-column layout with canvas on the left and control panel on the right (responsive down to 900px). Document the accordion-based control groups: Particles, Edges, Forces & Motion, Colors & Style, Interaction, Performance, and Presets/Import/Export. Explain the dynamic control generation in panel.js that binds UI elements to configuration values. Cover the keyboard shortcuts: Space (play/pause), R/Shift+R (reset), S (save PNG), [ / ] (count adjustment), and 1/2/3 (preset selection). Detail the accessibility features including labeled controls, title attributes, and keyboard navigation. Address the responsive design that moves the panel below canvas on smaller screens. Include information about the dark/light theme support via CSS variables and localStorage state persistence.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the public interfaces exposed by the application. For the event system, document config.on('change', callback) with parameters path (string) and value, explaining how to subscribe to configuration changes. Detail the export functions: exportJSON(config) returning a JSON string, importJSON(text) for configuration loading, savePNG(canvas) triggering download, and buildShareURL(config) generating URLs with encoded state. Document the preset system's getByName(name) function. For each API, specify parameters, return values, and provide usage examples. Include error handling strategies for invalid JSON or unsupported preset names. Address security considerations for input validation and rate limiting for export operations. Provide client implementation guidelines for integrating these APIs into external applications or custom scripts.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for developers contributing to the project. Provide setup instructions beyond basic installation, including development workflow and debugging techniques. Document the contribution process: forking the repository, creating feature branches, committing changes, and opening pull requests. Explain the code organization principles and coding standards expected for new contributions. Cover testing procedures and the MVP test checklist from tasks.md including parameter updates, reset functionality, import/export cycles, and performance targets. Address how to add new features while maintaining the vanilla JS architecture. Include guidelines for performance optimization and maintaining 60 FPS targets. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common development issues like event binding problems or rendering glitches.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Comprehensive Project Analysis: Plexus Canvas

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Plexus Canvas is a modern web application designed to visualize dynamic "plexus" networks consisting of particles and edges rendered on an HTML canvas. The application provides an interactive interface for real-time manipulation of network parameters, enabling users to explore various visual configurations and behaviors.

### Core Goals and Objectives
The primary objective of Plexus Canvas is to create a performant, interactive visualization tool that allows users to:
- Dynamically adjust particle and edge parameters in real-time
- Apply different visual presets for instant styling
- Export configurations and visual outputs in multiple formats
- Share customized configurations via URL parameters
- Maintain smooth performance (targeting 60 FPS) even with large particle counts

### Target Audience
The application targets developers, designers, and creative technologists interested in interactive visualizations, generative art, and network dynamics. It serves both as a standalone creative tool and as a codebase example for high-performance canvas rendering and state management.

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
Plexus Canvas follows a clean, modular architecture built with vanilla JavaScript (ES2020+) without any framework dependencies. The system is organized into distinct functional areas:

- **Rendering Engine**: Manages the animation loop and canvas context
- **State Management**: Handles configuration and change events
- **UI Components**: Dynamically generates controls and handles user interaction
- **Utility Functions**: Provides mathematical, DOM, and animation utilities
- **I/O Operations**: Manages import/export functionality

### Design Patterns
The application implements several key design patterns:

- **Event-Driven Architecture**: Configuration changes trigger updates across the system
- **Structure of Arrays (SoA)**: Particle data is stored in separate typed arrays for cache efficiency
- **Module Pattern**: Code is organized into focused, single-responsibility modules
- **Observer Pattern**: Configuration changes are broadcast to subscribers

### System Relationships
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Interface] --> B[Configuration State]
    B --> C[Rendering Engine]
    C --> D[Particle System]
    C --> E[Edge System]
    D --> F[Canvas Rendering]
    E --> F
    G[Keyboard Events] --> B
    H[Window Resize] --> C
    I[Import/Export] --> B
```

### Data Flow
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[UI Controls]
    B --> C[Config State]
    C --> D[Event Emission]
    D --> E[Renderer]
    D --> F[Particle System]
    D --> G[Edge System]
    E --> H[Canvas]
    F --> H
    G --> H
    H --> I[Visual Output]
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- [index.html](/index.html) - Primary HTML document containing canvas and control panel structure

### Core Modules
- [src/main.js](/src/main.js) - Application bootstrap and initialization
- [src/render/engine.js](/src/render/engine.js) - Rendering loop and time-step management
- [src/render/plexus.js](/src/render/plexus.js) - Particle and edge logic with spatial indexing
- [src/state/config.js](/src/state/config.js) - Configuration state management with validation
- [src/ui/panel.js](/src/ui/panel.js) - Dynamic control panel generation and binding

### Configuration Approach
- [src/state/presets.js](/src/state/presets.js) - Predefined configuration presets
- [aicontext/tasks.md](/aicontext/tasks.md) - Detailed configuration schema and parameter definitions

### External Dependencies
The project is designed to work without external dependencies, though `file-saver` can be optionally integrated for enhanced download functionality.

### Integration Points
- [src/io/exporter.js](/src/io/exporter.js) - JSON import/export, PNG saving, and share URL generation
- [src/utils/dom.js](/src/utils/dom.js) - DOM manipulation utilities
- [src/utils/raf.js](/src/utils/raf.js) - RequestAnimationFrame utilities

### Component Relationships
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[index.html] --> B[main.js]
    B --> C[config.js]
    B --> D[engine.js]
    B --> E[panel.js]
    C --> F[presets.js]
    C --> G[exporter.js]
    D --> H[plexus.js]
    E --> C
    F --> C
    G --> C
    H --> D
    I[Keyboard] --> B
    J[Resize] --> D
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
Plexus Canvas offers a comprehensive set of features for creating and manipulating particle network visualizations:

- Real-time parameter adjustment without page reload
- Multiple visualization presets for instant styling
- Full configuration import/export via JSON
- PNG image export of current visualization
- Shareable URLs containing encoded configuration
- Keyboard shortcuts for common actions
- Responsive design with mobile considerations

### Implementation Highlights
- [src/state/config.js](/src/state/config.js#L1-L50) - Event-driven configuration system with change notifications
- [src/render/plexus.js](/src/render/plexus.js#L1-L100) - Optimized particle system using Float32Array and spatial indexing
- [src/ui/panel.js](/src/ui/panel.js#L1-L80) - Dynamic UI generation based on configuration schema
- [src/io/exporter.js](/src/io/exporter.js#L1-L40) - Comprehensive import/export functionality including share URLs

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialization
    Initialization --> Idle: Load config from URL/localStorage
    Idle --> Rendering: Start animation loop
    Rendering --> Interactive: Accept user input
    Interactive --> ConfigurationChange: Parameter updated
    ConfigurationChange --> DebouncedUpdate: Apply changes with debounce
    DebouncedUpdate --> Rendering
    Interactive --> ExportAction: User requests export
    ExportAction --> FileOutput: Generate PNG/JSON
    FileOutput --> Interactive
    Interactive --> URLUpdate: Share configuration
    URLUpdate --> Interactive
    Idle --> Reset: User resets system
    Reset --> Initialization
```

Sources:
- [README.md](d:\DEV\plexus-canvas\README.md)
- [tasks.md](d:\DEV\plexus-canvas\aicontext\tasks.md)
</blog>
